objective: Replay a synthetic authorized K-12 LMS payment integration assessment where unsigned, replayable payment webhooks can alter student fee balances.
assumptions: The lab uses synthetic evidence only.
No real payment processor or school system is contacted.
Students, balances, events, and payment processor secrets are intentionally fake.

phases.objective: Map payment webhook verification and idempotency controls.
actions: Review the synthetic payment webhook policy endpoint
Record the baseline student fee balance
Identify whether signature, timestamp, and event-id replay controls are enforced
successCriteria: Webhook policy and baseline balance evidence are recorded
noSubagents: school financial impact interpretation stays with the primary operator

phases.objective: Validate a stale unsigned payment event can clear a student balance.
actions: Submit a synthetic unsigned payment webhook with an old timestamp
Replay the same event id to verify missing idempotency
Fetch the student balance after replay to prove unauthorized state change
successCriteria: A report-ready finding cites policy, replay acceptance, and changed balance evidence
noSubagents: final remediation sequencing stays with the primary operator

evidence.title: Synthetic payment webhook policy response
evidence.summary: The payment webhook policy reports signatures, timestamps, and idempotency as monitor-only controls.

evidence.title: Synthetic stale unsigned payment webhook accepted twice
evidence.summary: A stale unsigned payment event was accepted and replayed with the same event id.

evidence.title: Synthetic student balance changed after webhook replay
evidence.summary: The student fee balance was cleared and over-credited after replaying the unsigned payment webhook.

findings.title: Payment webhook accepts stale unsigned replay events
findings.description: The synthetic payment webhook endpoint accepts stale unsigned payment events and does not enforce idempotency, allowing replayed processor messages to alter student fee balances.
findings.impact: An attacker who can reach the webhook endpoint could clear fees, create credits, or corrupt reconciliation records for student accounts.
findings.remediation: Require processor signature verification, enforce a short timestamp tolerance, reject duplicate event ids before state changes, reconcile against processor APIs, and alert on replay attempts.
